indemnification.description: Clause requiring one or both parties to compensate the other for losses
critical_risk_signals: indemnify for gross negligence or willful misconduct of other party
indemnification.standard_market_position: Mutual indemnification for third-party IP infringement claims, personal injury, and property damage. Each party indemnifies for its own acts. Capped at fees paid in prior 12 months.
indemnification.jurisdiction_notes: California courts may limit scope of indemnification for active negligence

limitation_of_liability.description: Clause capping the maximum damages either party can recover
low_risk_signals: carve-outs for data breaches and IP
high_risk_signals: waiver of all consequential damages without carve-out
critical_risk_signals: no limitation of liability clause at all
limitation_of_liability.standard_market_position: Cap at 12 months of fees paid. Mutual exclusion of consequential/indirect damages. Carve-outs for: confidentiality breaches, data protection violations, gross negligence, willful misconduct.
limitation_of_liability.jurisdiction_notes: California law may not enforce limitation of liability for intentional acts or gross negligence

termination.description: Provisions governing when and how the agreement can be ended
low_risk_signals: termination for cause with cure period
critical_risk_signals: perpetual agreement with no termination right
termination.standard_market_position: Either party may terminate for convenience with 30-day notice. Termination for cause with 30-day cure period. Immediate termination rights for insolvency, material breach of confidentiality.
termination.jurisdiction_notes: California employment contracts may not include non-compete provisions triggered by termination

ip_assignment.description: Clause determining ownership of intellectual property created during the engagement
low_risk_signals: limited to work product specifically created for client
high_risk_signals: broad assignment of all IP including pre-existing work
assignment of IP created on own time
critical_risk_signals: assignment of all inventions including those unrelated to work
ip_assignment.standard_market_position: Contractor assigns only work product specifically created for this engagement. Background IP remains with creator. Client receives license to use background IP as needed for the work product.
ip_assignment.jurisdiction_notes: California Labor Code Section 2870 protects employees from IP assignment for inventions unrelated to employer's business

non_compete.description: Restrictions on competitive activities after the engagement ends
non_compete.standard_market_position: Non-solicitation of employees and customers is generally enforceable. Non-compete covenants are void and unenforceable in California under Business and Professions Code Section 16600.
non_compete.jurisdiction_notes: NON-COMPETE COVENANTS ARE VOID IN CALIFORNIA. Only limited exceptions for sale of business. Non-solicitation of employees may also be unenforceable.

high_risk_signals: no exceptions for publicly available information
overly broad definition of confidential information
critical_risk_signals: disclosure of confidential information to competitors allowed
confidentiality.standard_market_position: Mutual confidentiality. 2-3 year term (or perpetual for trade secrets). Standard exceptions: publicly known, independently developed, received from third party, required by law. Standard of care: reasonable care, at least same as own confidential information.
confidentiality.jurisdiction_notes: California Uniform Trade Secrets Act provides independent protection for trade secrets regardless of contract

governing_law.description: Choice of law and jurisdiction for dispute resolution
high_risk_signals: jurisdiction with unfavorable law for your claims
critical_risk_signals: foreign jurisdiction with no legal recourse
governing_law.standard_market_position: Each party should prefer its home jurisdiction. Delaware is common for corporate law matters. California for employment. New York for financial transactions.
governing_law.jurisdiction_notes: California courts will apply California law for California employees regardless of choice of law clause (Labor Code)

dispute_resolution.description: Process for resolving disagreements between parties
critical_risk_signals: class action waiver without adequate notice
arbitration clause that prevents injunctive relief
dispute_resolution.standard_market_position: Escalation: informal negotiation (30 days) → mediation → binding arbitration. AAA Commercial Rules. Arbitration in commercial center near both parties. Carve-out for injunctive relief in court.
dispute_resolution.jurisdiction_notes: California courts scrutinize arbitration agreements for unconscionability. Class action waivers in consumer contracts may be void.

data_protection.description: Obligations regarding collection, processing, and protection of personal data
high_risk_signals: broad license to use personal data
critical_risk_signals: sale or sharing of personal data with third parties
data_protection.standard_market_position: Comply with applicable privacy laws. Process data only as instructed. Security measures at least industry standard. Breach notification within 72 hours. Delete or return data on termination. DPA for EU/EEA data.
data_protection.jurisdiction_notes: California CCPA/CPRA applies to businesses meeting revenue/data thresholds. GDPR applies to EU data subjects regardless of company location.

force_majeure.description: Excuses performance for events beyond a party's reasonable control
critical_risk_signals: no force majeure provision in long-term contracts
force_majeure.standard_market_position: Covers: natural disasters, war, government action, pandemic. Not covered: economic hardship, market changes, supplier failures within control. Requires: prompt notice, duty to mitigate, termination right if exceeds 60-90 days.
force_majeure.jurisdiction_notes: California courts interpret force majeure clauses narrowly — only events specifically listed are covered

payment_terms.description: Terms governing when and how payment is made
high_risk_signals: disputed invoice process that allows indefinite delay
payment_terms.standard_market_position: Net 30 from invoice date. Interest on late payments at 1.5% per month or maximum legal rate. Dispute process for good-faith disputes with undisputed amounts paid on time.
payment_terms.jurisdiction_notes: California Prompt Payment Act applies to public contracts. Interest on late payments may be subject to usury laws.

warranty.description: Promises about the quality, fitness, or characteristics of goods/services
critical_risk_signals: warranty of fitness that cannot be met
warranty.standard_market_position: Services performed in professional manner consistent with industry standards. Software performs materially as documented. Warranty period: 90 days for software, 1 year for professional services. Remedy: re-perform or refund.
warranty.jurisdiction_notes: Implied warranties of merchantability and fitness for purpose apply under California Commercial Code unless expressly disclaimed
